Election.title: BV2217 — Pineapple progression 2/3 — Choose-One elects Pineapple on 25%, but STAR, Approval & Ranked Robin elect Cheese
Election.description: Rung 2/3 of the pineapple progression from the STAR education repo (github.com/masiarek/star-voting-library). A group shares ONE pizza; plain Cheese is nobody's favorite but everybody's easy second. With 5 toppings on the menu, Choose-One (mark your one favorite) crowns the biggest fan club, Pineapple, on just 25% — while STAR, Approval and Ranked Robin all read the whole ballot and elect Cheese, the Condorcet winner that beats every topping head-to-head. The more crowded the menu, the smaller Choose-One's winning share; the whole-ballot methods don't budge. Same lesson as the canonical 34% minority-winner case, one memorable pizza.

races.title: Choose-One (Plurality) — mark your one favorite topping
Approval — approve every topping you're fine with
Ranked Robin (Condorcet) — rank; every pair head-to-head
STAR — score 0-5, then an automatic runoff

logs: Pineapple has the most votes and wins the round
Cheese has the most approvals and wins the round
Cheese wins round with highest number of wins.
